finding: Historical queue records require an explicit compatibility layer so schema deviations can be reviewed without rewriting source history or silently normalizing ambiguity.

observed: The canonical queue is older than multiple append-only queue updates.
The effective-queue generator fails closed on missing identity and unresolved same-time conflicts.
No workflow run was returned for the inspected M-005 completion commit.
inferred: Historical records may contain schema deviations that should not be repaired in place.
A narrow manifest is safer than permissive parsing because tolerated defects remain visible.
proposed: Run every discovered queue update through the compatibility classifier before reconciliation.
Permit an exception only when every defect is enumerated, a rationale and reviewer are present, and a replacement record path is named.
superseded: Silently accepting heterogeneous historical queue records based only on parser tolerance.
unresolved: The complete historical queue-update corpus has not yet been executed through the classifier.
The first Continuity Effective Queue workflow result remains unavailable through the inspected commit-run lookup.

verification.source_persistence: GitHub returned commit identities for module and test files.

privacy_boundary: Public repository metadata and abstract operational claims only. Raw private chat, health and pet-health information, credentials, personal identifiers, and unreviewed uploads are excluded.
falsification_route: Execute the classifier against every operations/queue-updates JSON file. Any unmanifested defect, uncovered defect, expired exception, or incomplete review metadata must block effective-queue publication.
